questions.q: In which Indian city is the Golden Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Golden Temple stands in the city of Amritsar in the state of Punjab.

questions.q: The Golden Temple is the holiest shrine of which religion?
questions.explanation: Harmandir Sahib is the most sacred gurdwara and the spiritual centre of Sikhism.

questions.q: By what name is the Golden Temple also formally known?
questions.explanation: The shrine's formal name is Harmandir Sahib, also called Darbar Sahib, meaning the abode of God.

questions.q: In which Indian state is the Golden Temple situated?
questions.explanation: The Golden Temple is located in Amritsar, in the state of Punjab in north-western India.

questions.q: The Golden Temple sits in the middle of a sacred pool. What is this pool called?
questions.explanation: The temple is set in the Amrit Sarovar, the 'pool of nectar' that gives Amritsar its name.

questions.q: Why is the temple commonly called the 'Golden' Temple?
options: It is lit by golden lamps
Its upper levels are covered in gold
It was built by a king named Golden
questions.explanation: The upper floors and dome of the sanctum are covered in gold leaf and gilded copper, giving it its golden appearance.

questions.q: How many entrances does the Golden Temple's sanctum have, opening on all sides?
questions.explanation: The sanctum is open on all four sides, symbolising that the shrine welcomes people from every direction and background.

questions.q: What does the open, four-sided design of the Golden Temple symbolise?
options: Openness to people of all backgrounds
questions.explanation: The four open doors express that the gurdwara is open to all people regardless of caste, creed or origin.

questions.q: What free communal meal, served to all visitors, is a famous tradition at the Golden Temple?
questions.explanation: The Golden Temple runs a langar, a free community kitchen that serves vegetarian meals to tens of thousands of people every day.

questions.q: Which holy book of the Sikhs is installed in the sanctum of the Golden Temple?
questions.explanation: The Guru Granth Sahib, the central Sikh scripture, is enshrined and read in the Golden Temple's sanctum.

questions.q: Which Sikh Guru is credited with founding the city of Amritsar and excavating the sacred pool?
questions.explanation: Guru Ram Das, the fourth Sikh Guru, founded the settlement around the sacred tank in the late 16th century.

questions.q: Which Sikh Guru oversaw the building of the Harmandir Sahib temple itself?
questions.explanation: Guru Arjan, the fifth Guru, had the temple constructed and installed the first Adi Granth there in 1604.

questions.q: Approximately when was the Harmandir Sahib temple completed and the scripture first installed?
questions.explanation: The temple was completed in the early 1600s, with the Adi Granth installed by Guru Arjan in 1604.

questions.q: Which Sikh ruler is famous for adorning the temple's upper levels with gold in the early 19th century?
questions.explanation: Maharaja Ranjit Singh, ruler of the Sikh Empire, had the upper structure covered in gold and marble in the early 1800s.

questions.q: Unlike most temples set on raised platforms, the Golden Temple was built at a lower level. What does this symbolise?
questions.explanation: Worshippers descend steps to reach the temple, a design meant to teach humility before God.

questions.q: The throne of Sikh temporal authority faces the Golden Temple across the complex. What is it called?
questions.explanation: The Akal Takht, the highest seat of Sikh temporal authority, stands opposite the sanctum within the complex.

questions.q: What is the name of the causeway that connects the entrance to the sanctum across the pool?
questions.explanation: A marble causeway leads from the Darshani Deori archway across the Amrit Sarovar to the central sanctum.

questions.q: What does the name 'Amritsar', the city of the Golden Temple, mean?
questions.explanation: Amritsar means 'pool of the nectar of immortality', referring to the sacred Amrit Sarovar around the temple.

questions.q: The architecture of the Golden Temple is noted for blending which two styles?
questions.explanation: The temple fuses Mughal and Hindu architectural elements into a distinctive Sikh style with a domed sanctum.

questions.q: What is the principal precious material used to gild the temple's dome and upper walls?
questions.explanation: Gold leaf over gilded copper covers the dome and upper portions, giving the shrine its gleaming surface.

questions.q: What is the white stone of which the lower walls and surrounding plaza are largely built?
questions.explanation: White marble forms much of the lower walls and the broad parikrama (walkway) around the pool.

questions.q: Which musical tradition is continuously performed at the Golden Temple as part of worship?
questions.explanation: Kirtan, the devotional singing of hymns from the Guru Granth Sahib, is performed almost continuously at the shrine.

questions.q: According to tradition, who is said to have laid the foundation stone of the Harmandir Sahib?
questions.explanation: By tradition the Muslim Sufi saint Mian Mir of Lahore laid the foundation stone, reflecting the shrine's inclusive ethos.

questions.q: The scripture installed in 1604 by Guru Arjan was originally known by which name?
questions.explanation: Guru Arjan compiled and installed the Adi Granth, the early version of what later became the Guru Granth Sahib.

questions.q: The Golden Temple complex was extensively damaged during which 1762 event involving an Afghan invader?
options: The sack by Ahmad Shah Abdali
questions.explanation: Afghan invader Ahmad Shah Abdali (Durrani) blew up and desecrated the temple in 1762, after which the Sikhs rebuilt it.

questions.q: The shrine within the complex housing a smaller tower associated with Guru Hargobind's young son is known as?
questions.explanation: Baba Atal Sahib, a tall tower-gurdwara near the complex, commemorates Atal Rai, the son of Guru Hargobind.

questions.q: In June 1984 the Golden Temple complex was the site of which Indian Army operation?
questions.explanation: Operation Blue Star was the Indian Army action at the complex in June 1984, which damaged the Akal Takht.

questions.q: The fourth Guru, who founded Amritsar, obtained the land for the tank during whose reign as Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: The site was developed during the reign of Akbar, who was a contemporary and reportedly favourable to the Sikh Gurus.

questions.q: The lotus-shaped inverted dome of the Golden Temple is most often described in which form?
options: An inverted lotus topped by a finial
questions.explanation: The sanctum is crowned by a gilded dome shaped like an inverted lotus, a hallmark of its distinctive silhouette.

questions.q: The body of water around the Golden Temple is fed by a channel drawing from which major Punjab river system?
options: The Ravi (via canal from the upper rivers)
questions.explanation: The Amrit Sarovar is supplied by a canal channel connected to Punjab's river system, historically associated with the Ravi.
